content:
# D-Log M Saturation in Linear Space is Very High
Underwater footage decoded from D-Log M to linear light has pixel saturation of ~0.85 (computed as (max-min)/max per pixel in linear RGB). This is because red absorption at depth creates extreme R:B channel ratios. A saturation filter threshold below 0.85 will reject most underwater pixels, making grey-world WB estimation impossible. Threshold must be >= 0.90 for underwater D-Log M footage.
